summary: Matched the item capsule translation unit by implementing the previously NOT_IMPLEMENTED it_8027D530, cleaning stack-padding artifacts in nearby capsule callbacks, and flipping src/melee/it/items/itcapsule.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The decomp.dev report credited 2 new function matches in main/melee/it/items/itcapsule: it_8027D530 reached 100.00% from 1.75%, and it_8027D338 reached 100.00% from 76.36%, for +448 matched bytes and +1904 linked bytes.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itcapsule.c") replaces the previous NonMatching entry for the capsule item translation unit.
In it_8027CF30, split the chained access it->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes into an Article* article temporary followed by ItCapsuleAttr* attr = article->x4_specialAttributes.
In it_8027CF30, reordered locals so Item* and Article*/attr locals precede Vec3 sp18 and the u8 padding array, and removed zero-initialization from the padding array.
Removed an unused u8 _[4] = { 0 } stack-padding local from it_8027D338.
Implemented it_8027D530: it gets the Item from the item GObj, checks it->msid == 3, calls it_8027CF30(item_gobj), returns it->xDD4_itemVar.capsule.x0 in that case, and returns false otherwise.
smart_moves: Used a small Article* temporary in it_8027CF30 to influence codegen for an otherwise equivalent nested field access, a common matching tactic when load scheduling or register allocation differs.
Treated stack filler deliberately: removing zero-initializers and deleting unnecessary padding changed stack/codegen without altering meaningful behavior.
Kept it_8027D530 minimal and behavior-driven, matching the apparent item state guard on msid == 3 before invoking the shared capsule helper it_8027CF30.
Only flipped the configure.py object to Matching after the unit-level matching report showed capsule functions reaching 100%.
decomp_lessons: For item files, full TU matching may depend on very small local declaration and stack-layout changes in already-decompiled helper functions, not only on implementing missing functions.
When nested struct accesses mismatch, introducing an explicitly typed intermediate such as Article* can be enough to reproduce the original compiler's load/register pattern.
Anonymous u8 padding arrays should not be initialized unless the target code really emits stores for them; zero-initialized dummy padding can prevent a match.
A previously NOT_IMPLEMENTED function can sometimes be reconstructed as a short state-gated wrapper around an existing helper plus an item-var return value.
After an item translation unit is matched, remember to move its configure.py object from NonMatching to Matching so linked/matched metrics reflect the result.
naming_conventions: Address-style symbol names were retained: it_8027CF30, it_8027D338, it_8027D530.
Item object parameters use Item_GObj* or HSD_GObj* and names like item_gobj/gobj, matching existing item-module style.
Item data is accessed through GET_ITEM(item_gobj) into an Item* local named it.
Capsule-specific item variables are accessed through it->xDD4_itemVar.capsule.x0.
Dummy stack padding, when still needed, is represented as an anonymous-looking local named _ with type u8[].
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reordered local declarations in it_8027CF30 to affect stack layout and register allocation.
Removed zero-initialization from a dummy u8 padding array to avoid extra initialization stores.
Removed an unnecessary dummy u8 padding local from it_8027D338, which helped that function reach 100%.
Introduced an Article* intermediate for xC4_article_data before loading x4_specialAttributes.
Replaced NOT_IMPLEMENTED in it_8027D530 with a compact branch structure: compare msid to 3, call it_8027CF30 only on the true path, return the capsule flag, otherwise return false.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed matched code increasing to 29.36% (+0.01%, +448 bytes) and linked code increasing to 21.59% (+0.05%, +1904 bytes).
Automated report listed 2 new matches in main/melee/it/items/itcapsule: it_8027D530 (+225 bytes, 1.75% to 100.00%) and it_8027D338 (+53 bytes, 76.36% to 100.00%).
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs where a translation unit was moved from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py after removing NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs.
Search for other matches that introduced Article* temporaries to fix nested xC4_article_data access codegen.
Look for item functions where dummy u8 padding arrays or zero-initialized padding affected matching.
Compare it_8027D530 patterns with other item callbacks that check it->msid before calling a shared helper and returning an item-var flag.
